subject: Geology
title: What Are Igneous Rocks?
description: A quick, practical guide to What Are Igneous Rocks?.

hook.text: Liquid rock cools and freezes into stone. Every igneous rock began its life as magma or lava.

buildup.text: Igneous means 'from fire.' These rocks form when molten material solidifies, either underground or at the surface.

discovery.text: Intrusive igneous rocks cool slowly underground, forming large crystals. Extrusive ones cool fast, making tiny ones.

twist.text: Igneous rocks are the original crust-builders. All other rock types ultimately trace back to igneous origins.

climax.text: From granite countertops to volcanic islands, igneous rocks are the foundation of Earth's solid surface.

punchline.text: Every rock's ancestry leads back to fire.

quiz.question: How do igneous rocks form?
options: From heat and pressure on existing rock
